data.title: Perpetually grounded or ready to fly? The mystery of Tesla's next-gen Roadster
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) appears to still be moving forward with plans for a next-gen Roadster. Of course, the Roadster was the company’s first production car. The model was sold from 2008 to 2012 and is widely considered an important proof-of-concept with consumers that a lithium-ion battery sports car could deliver both real-world range and high performance. 

Built on a heavily modified Lotus Elise chassis with an AC induction motor and a roughly 200-mile real-world range, it sold in limited numbers at prices around and above six figures, with total production of about 2,450 units before the program ended as Tesla (TSLA) shifted focus to the Model S. A second-generation Roadster was first unveiled as a prototype in 2017 as an all-electric supercar promising sub-2-second 0–60 mph acceleration, more than 250 mph top speed, and a claimed range around 620 miles from a roughly 200 kWh battery pack. However, Tesla (TSLA) has repeatedly delayed the new Roadster, citing the need to prioritize higher-volume vehicles and the maturation of advanced battery technology.

Design leaders at the electric vehicle company have described the Roadster project as a kind of "racing program" intended to push engineering limits and showcase new technology, although that explanation has not satisfied fans of the Roadster who made deposits years ago.

Recent statements and reports indicate that development work on the new Roadster may have ramped back up in 2025. Elon Musk has now tied the reveal of a production-ready version to an event scheduled for April 1, 2026, and has suggested production could start roughly 12–18 months after that, implying a possible 2027–2028 start if the schedule holds. However, the April 1 timing of the event has some analysts warning of an April Fools’ Day prank. 

Last month, Elon Musk said Tesla (TSLA) was nearing the point of showing the updated Roadster prototype, describing the planned demo as potentially the most memorable or "most mind-blowing" product reveal ever. He has even hinted at a flying-car-style capability for the next-gen Roadster. "We need to make sure that it works. This is some crazy technology in this car. Let’s just put it this way: if you took all the James Bond cars and combined them, it’s crazier than that," he told Joe Rogan.

XPeng (XPEV) and Hyundai (HYMTF) are the other car companies that are reported to be working on electric vertical takeoff and landing flying car concepts.

data.title: Is the stock market open on Thanksgiving and Black Friday?
data.description:
Investors navigating a turbulent November so far will get some time off from the stock market on Thanksgiving, as Wall Street is closed. Equity index futures (SPX)(INDU)(US100:IND) will be active, however, and for those looking to trade, crypto will be available all day. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) closed at 6,812.61 points on Wednesday, posting a four-day win streak and consolidating a strong rebound from what has been a tough month. The benchmark index had fallen -4.4% in November before that four-day run, as sentiment was battered by weakness in the artificial intelligence trade and whipsawing rate cut expectations.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended at 23,214.69 points on Wednesday, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) concluded at 47,427.12 points. 

Looking at some history, the first Thanksgiving between the Mayflower pilgrims, who founded the Plymouth Colony in Massachusetts, and the Wampanoag tribe is said to have taken place in 1621.

Thanksgiving proclamations were made in subsequent years, and in 1789, George Washington declared November 26 "as a day of public thanksgiving and prayer, to be observed by acknowledging with grateful hearts the many and signal favors of Almighty God."

Americans still observed the holiday on differing dates, so beginning in 1863, President Lincoln encouraged Americans to recognize the last Thursday of November as "a day of Thanksgiving." A few years later, in 1870, Congress followed suit by passing legislation making Thanksgiving a national holiday.

As for Black Friday, the stock market will open as usual at 0930 ET but will shut early, with low volumes and trading activity typically seen until the close at 1300 ET. Bond markets will also close early at 1400 ET.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

Don't forget to check out Seeking Alpha's comprehensive guide to Stock Market Holidays.

More on Thanksgiving and Black Friday

  • Thanksgiving feast of gains: 10 best S&P 500 stocks of 2025
  • Thanksgiving leftovers no one wants: 10 worst S&P 500 stocks of 2025
  • 10 airline stocks to watch as Thanksgiving travel takes off
  • Record number of shoppers expected to turn out during Thanksgiving weekend -- NRF

data.title: Wall Street finished higher on fresh economic signals ahead of Thanksgiving
data.description:
Wall Street ended higher on Wednesday, with traders and investors digesting new economic data amid ongoing rate-cut speculation.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) which rallied north of 650 points on Tuesday closed higher by 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended on top by 0.6% and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced by 0.8%.

Looking at the S&P 500 by sector, nine of the 11 segments finished the session higher, with Utilities leading the way. To the downside, Communication Services was the weakest performing area.

“There seems to be a lot of optimism centered around the prospect of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in December. If that comes to pass, it would definitely prove bullish since cheap money makes investment in growth more attractive,” Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones stated.

Treasury yields were mixed as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved lower by 1 basis point to 3.99% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.47%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims surprised lower in the week ended November 22. Durable goods orders slowed in September, as expected. 

Also, the Federal Reserve's Beige Book report highlighted that most of the Federal Reserve's 12 districts said U.S. economic activity was "little changed" in recent weeks.

“Stocks are celebrating the likelihood of a December rate cut, as it's becoming clear that the labor market is the priority for the Federal Reserve, and another rate cut would help to protect against any further cooling of the job market,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated. 

“It's becoming more clear that the stock market declines seen over most of November were simply a pullback after an extremely strong run in October and September,” Stanley added. 

As for stocks that were are on the move, shares of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) ended higher by 10.9% while shares of Zscaler (ZS) dropped 12.8%.

For reference, markets will be closed on November 27, Thursday, for the Thanksgiving holiday.

More on markets

  • Thanksgiving leftovers no one wants: 10 worst S&P 500 stocks of 2025
  • Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and 10 retail stocks to watch
  • Don't Buy This Dip: It's Black Friday In The Equity Market
  • AI Spending War And AI Debt Pile-Up Could Squeeze Share Buybacks

data.title: U.S. oil rig count tumbles to lowest in more than four years, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. fell by 10 to 544, after rising for five out of the previous six weeks, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly report.

In the shortened week ending November 26, drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. fell by 12 from last week to 407, its lowest since September 2021, but gas rigs gained 3 to 130, its highest since July 2023, and 7 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

The total count was down 38, or 6.5%, from the same time last year.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin fell by 3 to 251, the Williston Basin was unchanged at 29, and the Eagle Ford fell by 2 to 26.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Atlanta Fed's Q3 GDP estimate edges down after durable goods report
data.description:
The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model estimates Q3 growth of 3.9%, on a seasonally adjusted annual rate, a tick down from its previous estimate of 4.0% as of Nov. 25, the district Federal Reserve bank said Wednesday.

The latest estimate incorporated data from September's durable goods manufacturing report from the U.S. Census Bureau. The model's nowcast of Q3 real gross private domestic investment growth dropped to 3.5% from 4.4%.

GDPNow isn't a forecast, but a running estimate of real GDP growth based on economic data available for the current quarter.

data.title: APA, Coterra, Ovintiv started Outperform at William Blair, citing strong free cash flow
data.description:
APA Corp. (APA), Coterra Energy (CTRA) and Ovintiv (OVV) +3.7%, +2.8% and +4.4% respectively in Wednesday's trading, along with most oil and gas stocks as crude oil rebounded from one-month lows, as William Blair analyst Neal Dingmann initiated coverage of all three names to Outperform.

APA's (APA) Permian asset quality has been criticized by some investors, but Dingmann believes the size of the position, combined with several other large operating areas, presents a unique investment, pointing to notable upside potential from Egypt's natural gas acreage given prior unexplored development and much-improved pricing structure, and Suriname's estimated recoverable resource, with drilling expected late next year; the company should generate nearly $1.4 billion in free cash flow in 2026 and $1.7 billion in 2027, the analyst said.

Coterra (CTRA) continues to demonstrate operational efficiencies in the Delaware Basin through its Windham Row project, where it operates 73 gross wells across six drilling spacing units, and the 28-gross-well Barba Row project and the 48-gross-well Bowler Row project will be coming online soon, Dingmann said, estimating the company should generate ~$2.9 billion free cash flow in 2026 and $3.9 billion in 2027, assuming oil and natural gas prices stay relatively flat in 2026 and then increase in 2027.

Ovintiv (OVV) has put together a solid asset base focusing on the Permian Basin and Montney formation, and while the company has organically and externally grown production/reserves, focus also has remained on the balance sheet with a goal of less than $4 billion in net debt likely after a potential sale of the Mid-Continent position, according to Dingmann, who sees the company generating $1.7 billion in free cash flow in 2026 and $2.4 billion in 2027.

data.title: Campbell's fires employee who went on a wild rant disparaging its food and customers
data.description:
The Campbell's Company (CPB) indicated in a statement issued late on Wednesday that the food giant no longer employs an executive who was allegedly recorded criticizing the company’s food and making racist remarks.

"After a review, we believe the voice on the recording is in fact Martin Bally," read part of the update from the company. "The comments were vulgar, offensive, and false, and we apologize for the hurt they have caused."

Bally, who was a vice president of information technology at Campbell's (CPB), complained that the company’s products were highly processed food for poor people and referred to Indian co-workers as idiots. The comments became public as part of a lawsuit filed by Robert Garza, a former Campbell's (PCB) employee. Garza also made a wild claim that Campbell's (CPB) created chicken from 3-D printers, which was strongly refuted by Campbell's (CPB).

Campbell's (CPB) made it clear that Bally's comments don’t reflect its values or culture and that type of language will not be tolerated.

The employee flare-up did not impact shares of Campbell's (CPB), which slipped 0.2% on trading volume below normal activity.

data.title: Otsuka gains FDA approval for sibeprenlimab for proteinuria reduction
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA has approved Otsuka Pharmaceutical's (OTCPK:OTSKF) Voyxact (sibeprenlimab) for proteinuria reduction in individuals with primary immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN).
  • The drug is dosed every four weeks as a self-administered, subcutaneous injection.
  • Approval was based on results from an interim analysis of the VISIONARY Phase 3 trial. Results showed a placebo-adjusted 51% reduction in proteinuria at nine months.
  • Earlier in the week, Otsuka submitted an NDA to the agency for centanafadine, its once-daily norepinephrine, dopamine, and serotonin reuptake inhibitor (NDSRI) for ADHD. That application is backed by four phase 3 trials.

data.title: SA Quant ranks Goldman's large-cap stocks with largest short interest
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analysts last week identified 50 large-cap stocks with the highest short interest as a percentage of market cap, a list that was topped by Bloom Energy (BE).

The brokerage looked at the short interest as of October 31, 2025, in companies over $25B in market cap, with each stock owned by an average of 65 hedge funds.

Seeking Alpha ranked Goldman's list by its Quant Rating system. Seagate Technology (STX) and Synchrony Financial (SYF) emerged as the only two Strong Buys, while Dell Technologies (DELL) just missed that threshold. Workday (WDAY) and Realty Income (O) rounded out the top five, each maintaining Buy ratings despite varying year-to-date performances. Royal Caribbean (RCL) and FirstEnergy (FE) also appeared in the upper tier with solid Buy ratings.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system evaluates stocks based on their performance across critical quantitative measures including valuation, growth, profitability, and momentum metrics. Ratings range from 1 to 5, with ratings above 3.5 considered bullish (buy or strong buy), and ratings below 2.5 deemed bearish (sell or strong sell).

Here are the top 20 stocks of Goldman's 50, ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant rating:

  1. Seagate Technology (STX), Quant Rating: 4.98, Strong Buy
  2. Synchrony Financial (SYF), Quant Rating: 4.82, Strong Buy
  3. Dell Technologies (DELL), Quant Rating: 4.20, Buy
  4. Workday (WDAY), Quant Rating: 3.86, Buy
  5. Realty Income (O), Quant Rating: 3.79, Buy
  6. Royal Caribbean (RCL), Quant Rating: 3.66, Buy
  7. FirstEnergy (FE), Quant Rating: 3.63, Buy
  8. Credo Technology (CRDO), Quant Rating: 3.49, Hold
  9. Bloom Energy (BE), Quant Rating: 3.49, Hold
  10. Robinhood Markets (HOOD), Quant Rating: 3.48, Hold
  11. SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Quant Rating: 3.47, Hold
  12. Reddit (RDDT), Quant Rating: 3.43, Hold
  13. Teradyne (TER), Quant Rating: 3.43, Hold
  14. Expedia (EXPE), Quant Rating: 3.42, Hold
  15. Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO), Quant Rating: 3.39, Hold
  16. MongoDB (MDB), Quant Rating: 3.37, Hold
  17. Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold
  18. Snowflake (SNOW), Quant Rating: 3.35, Hold
  19. Ford (F), Quant Rating: 3.35, Hold
  20. PayPal (PYPL), Quant Rating: 3.33, Hold

data.title: SA Quant ranks Goldman's small-cap stocks with largest short interest
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analysts last week identified 50 small-cap stocks with the highest short interest as a percentage of market cap, a list that was topped by Anavex Life Sciences (AVXL).

The brokerage looked at the short interest as of October 31, 2025, in companies under $1B in market cap, with each stock owned by an average of 26 hedge funds. Goldman also looked at the highest short interest in large-cap stocks, a list that can be seen here. 

Seeking Alpha ranked Goldman's small-cap list by its Quant Rating system. AnaptysBio (ANAB) topped the rankings with a Strong Buy rating of 4.89 and an impressive year-to-date performance of 213.44%. Neurogene (NGNE) follows with a Buy rating of 4.00, though it shows a YTD performance of -9.27%. The majority of stocks on the list fall into the Hold category, including Bit Digital (BTBT), Cable One (CABO), and CorMedix (CRMD), with ratings between 3.42 and 3.21.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system rates stocks based on their performance across key quantitative metrics including valuation, growth, profitability, and momentum. Ratings range from 1 to 5, with scores above 3.5 considered bullish (Buy or Strong Buy), while ratings of 2.5 or below indicate bearish sentiment (Sell or Strong Sell).

Here are the top 20 stocks of Goldman's 50, ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant rating:
